task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened and read the technical rider.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or file viewer displaying the contents of '/home/ga/Audio/gig_info/tech_rider.txt'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required input tracks have been created in the Ardour session.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Editor or Mixer window shows audio tracks specifically named: 'Kick Drum', 'Drum Overheads', 'Upright Bass DI', 'Piano Left', 'Piano Right', 'Tenor Sax', and 'Room Mics'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required bus tracks have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Editor or Mixer window shows bus tracks specifically named 'Drum Sub' and 'Piano Sub'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the pan positions for the Piano and Saxophone tracks are adjusted according to the rider.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour Mixer view, the pan knobs for specific tracks are adjusted: 'Piano Left' is panned significantly to the left, 'Piano Right' is panned significantly to the right, and 'Tenor Sax' is panned slightly to the right.

task_completion.description: Verify that the gain staging for the bus tracks is set to -6 dB.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Ardour Mixer view, the volume faders for the 'Drum Sub' and 'Piano Sub' tracks are lowered, with the numerical readout showing approximately '-6.0' or '-6'.

task_completion.description: Verify that set list markers have been added to the session timeline.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Ardour Editor window shows location markers/flags along the top timeline ruler with names like 'Set 1 - Autumn Leaves', 'Blue in Green', 'All Blues', 'Set Break', 'Set 2 - My Favorite Things', 'Giant Steps', or 'Encore - Take Five'.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the Ardour GUI to set up the session.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Ardour application interface being actively used (menus opened, dialog boxes for creating tracks, mixer adjustments), rather than the agent just editing the underlying .ardour XML session file in a terminal text editor.

integrity.description: Verify that tracks and buses are genuinely created in the DAW.
integrity.visual_evidence: Track names, faders, and pan knobs must appear within the structural UI elements of the Ardour Editor or Mixer windows, not just typed out in a text document or notes app.

integrity.description: Verify that markers are placed on the actual Ardour timeline.
integrity.visual_evidence: Marker text must appear in the dedicated marker ruler/lane at the top of the Ardour Editor window, attached to specific timeline positions.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Required input tracks: 'Kick Drum', 'Drum Overheads', 'Upright Bass DI', 'Piano Left', 'Piano Right', 'Tenor Sax', 'Room Mics'. Required bus tracks: 'Drum Sub', 'Piano Sub'. Pan positions (where 0.5 is center): Piano Left at 0.15 (panned left), Piano Right at 0.85 (panned right), Tenor Sax at 0.65 (panned slightly right). Bus gain: -6 dB for both sub buses. Set list markers to look for on the timeline: 'Set 1 - Autumn Leaves', 'Blue in Green', 'All Blues', 'Set Break', 'Set 2 - My Favorite Things', 'Giant Steps', 'Encore - Take Five'.
